examples.title: Basic Class and Object Declaration
examples.description: Demonstrates how to define a class with attributes, methods, and create objects in C++
examples.explanation: This code demonstrates: (1) Class as blueprint with attributes (length, width) and methods, (2) Encapsulation using private/public specifiers, (3) Constructor for initialization, (4) Destructor for cleanup, (5) Objects as instances of class

examples.title: Inheritance Demonstration
examples.description: Shows how inheritance works in OOP - child class inherits from parent class
examples.code:
CLASS Person:
 ATTRIBUTES:
 name: String
 age: Integer
 METHODS:
 displayInfo():
 PRINT "Name: " + name
 PRINT "Age: " + age

CLASS Student EXTENDS Person: // Inheritance
 ATTRIBUTES:
 rollNo: String
 grade: Char
 METHODS:
 displayInfo(): // Method overriding (Polymorphism)
 CALL SUPER.displayInfo()
 PRINT "Roll No: " + rollNo
 PRINT "Grade: " + grade

BEGIN
 // Create objects
 Person p1
 p1.name = "John"
 p1.age = 35
 
 Student s1
 s1.name = "Alice" // Inherited from Person
 s1.age = 20 // Inherited from Person
 s1.rollNo = "CS001"
 s1.grade = 'A'
 
 // Polymorphism demonstration
 p1.displayInfo()
 s1.displayInfo() // Calls overridden method
END
examples.explanation: Demonstrates: (1) Inheritance - Student class inherits name, age from Person, (2) Code reusability - don't need to redefine name/age, (3) Polymorphism - displayInfo() behaves differently for Student

examples.title: Encapsulation Demonstration
examples.description: Shows how encapsulation protects data through access modifiers
examples.code:
CLASS BankAccount:
 PRIVATE ATTRIBUTES: // Encapsulation - data hidden
 accountNumber: String
 balance: Real
 pin: Integer
 
 PUBLIC METHODS:
 CONSTRUCTOR(accNo, initialBalance):
 accountNumber = accNo
 balance = initialBalance
 pin = 0
 
 deposit(amount):
 IF amount > 0 THEN
 balance = balance + amount
 PRINT "Deposited: " + amount
 RETURN balance
 ELSE
 PRINT "Invalid amount"
 RETURN -1
 
 withdraw(amount, enteredPin):
 IF enteredPin = pin THEN
 IF amount <= balance THEN
 balance = balance - amount
 PRINT "Withdrawn: " + amount
 RETURN balance
 ELSE
 PRINT "Insufficient funds"
 ELSE
 PRINT "Invalid PIN"
 
 getBalance(enteredPin):
 IF enteredPin = pin THEN
 RETURN balance
 ELSE
 PRINT "Access denied"

BEGIN
 account = BankAccount("ACC123", 10000)
 account.deposit(5000) // Allowed
 balance = account.getBalance(1234) // Error - wrong PIN
 account.balance = 1000000 // ERROR - Private, cannot access
END
examples.explanation: This demonstrates encapsulation: (1) Private attributes prevent direct access, (2) Public methods provide controlled access, (3) Validation in methods ensures data integrity, (4) Internal implementation hidden from outside
